purpose: Transparent decorative Stone Quarry art for the prepared, unmounted Stone Quarry inspection card

projectAuthorization.scope: Warpkeep draft PR #59 preparation and use of the background-cleaned derivative in the public Warpkeep GitHub repository, plus an eventual official warpkeep.com Pages runtime only after separately approved deployment. This is use authorization only, not deployment approval.
notGranted: general third-party derivative or redistribution permission
Stone-site placement, renderer, server, browser-derived balance, gathering, reward, or entitlement authority

generation.service: OpenAI image generation edit (built-in Codex imagegen)
generation.prompt:
Use case: background-extraction
Asset type: high-resolution in-game Stone Quarry inspection-panel artwork.
Input image: Image 1 is the edit target and visual reference.
Primary request: Preserve the supplied stylized 3D Stone Quarry illustration exactly in spirit and composition: stacked pale stone blocks, timber quarry hoist, small mine cart, workbench, teal sign, grass-and-dirt base, and warm low poly game-material style. Create a crisp, polished, high-detail square UI asset based on it.
Scene/backdrop: replace only the opaque black background with a perfectly flat solid #00ff00 chroma-key background for later background removal. No ground plane outside the illustrated base.
Composition/framing: centered 3/4 isometric view, entire quarry and base visible, generous even padding, no cropping.
Lighting/mood: retain soft studio daylight with clean pale stone and warm timber.
Constraints: keep the quarry recognizable and do not add people, buildings, text, logos, badges, watermark, UI frame, or new game symbols. The #00ff00 background must be one uniform color with no shadow, gradient, texture, reflection, floor, or lighting variation. Do not use #00ff00 in the quarry itself.

runtimeAsset.delivery: same-origin checked-in runtime asset; no external CDN

presentationBoundary.currentRealmMount: not mounted; a separate reviewed authoritative Stone-site integration is required

licence.policy: The exact project instruction authorizes this Warpkeep runtime use but does not establish a public relicensing grant. See ASSETS-LICENSE.md.
